text.title: Budget Reallocation Decisions
text.summary: Decision matrix by CPA vs target, scaling increment rules, and diminishing returns model.

list.item: CPA 1.0-1.3x target: hold budget, optimize creative
CPA above 2.0x target: pause, rethink strategy

rule.statement: Do not cut a campaign below its minimum learning threshold (typically $500-1000/month) before giving it at least 2 weeks of data at that level.
rule.rationale: Premature cuts eliminate campaigns that are still in the learning phase — the algorithm needs time to find efficient delivery patterns before performance stabilizes.

rule.statement: Never increase a campaign budget by more than 30% in a single step; wait 3-5 days before the next increase.
rule.rationale: Large budget jumps force automated bidding back into learning mode, causing CPA spikes of 20-80% for 3-7 days while the algorithm re-calibrates delivery.

section.title: Diminishing Returns Model
section.p: Expected CPA increase when scaling a single campaign:

rule.statement: When CPA increase exceeds 40% at any budget level, stop scaling that campaign and launch a new parallel campaign targeting a different audience segment instead.
rule.rationale: Beyond the diminishing-returns inflection point, adding budget to an existing campaign is less efficient than expanding the addressable audience via a new campaign.

example.code:
Expected spend on day X = Monthly budget * (Day / Days in month) Variance = Actual spend / Expected spend
Above 1.05: spending too fast, reduce daily budget 0.95-1.05: on track Below 0.95: underspending, check delivery issues
